title: Kaity Cimo is running the Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer’s Association with her mother in mind
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Kaity Cimo from Scituate, Mass. is running the Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer's Association.
Cimo's participation honors her mother, who is affected by Alzheimer's and expressed love through cooking.
Memories of her mother's cooking motivate Cimo, as she sorts through her mother's kitchen belongings.

title: On eve of trade deadline, Bruins make a horrid case for becoming buyers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins faced a setback after a 6-3 loss to the rebuilding Nashville Predators, affecting trade deadline decisions.
Boston's road record is subpar at 11-14-4, impacting their playoff positioning and trade deadline strategies.
Don Sweeney considers cautious trade moves, weighing future assets against immediate roster upgrades before the deadline.

title: Judge pauses deportation of Boston area Irish man held in custody for 6 months
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Judge David Barron halted Seamus Culleton's deportation, pending resolution of his review petition; he remains detained in Texas.
Culleton overstayed a 90-day visa in 2009 and spoke out about conditions at ERO East Montana ICE Detention Camp.
Culleton's lawyer argues for his right to stay in the U.S. and reunite with his Boston-based spouse.

title: Victim in early morning Foxborough fire identified
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Fire claimed the life of 81-year-old George Schubert and his dog on Granite Street, Foxborough.
The fire began around 3 a.m. Tuesday; firefighters from Mansfield and Plainville assisted Foxborough units.
The blaze caused severe damage to the home; the fire's cause is still under investigation.

title: Boston tree canopy has expanded, in step with plans for a cleaner, greener city
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston's tree canopy expanded by 151 acres between 2019 and 2024, increasing the city's coverage to 28.5%.
The city's tree growth primarily occurred in public parks and rights-of-way, contrasting with no net change from the previous period.
East Boston, Jamaica Plain, South Boston, and West Roxbury saw net gains; Hyde Park and Mattapan experienced net losses.

title: Kraft agrees to help cover World Cup security costs, but Foxbourough leaders still unsatisfied
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Kraft Sports & Entertainment agrees to help cover $7.8 million in World Cup security costs at Gillette Stadium.
Foxborough town officials remain unsatisfied, stating Kraft's commitments do not fully address their operational concerns.
Foxborough must decide by March 17 whether to grant an event license for seven World Cup games at the stadium.

title: Two Saints Tavern, a familiar bar for Northeastern students, is closing after 10 years in Fenway
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two Saints Tavern in Fenway will close this weekend after nearly a decade of operation.
Managing partner Chuck Hitchcock cited rising insurance costs as the main reason for the bar's closure.
Two Saints Tavern made 85% of sales from liquor, with insurance costs increasing from $18,000 to $75,000-$82,000 annually.

title: 4 questions facing Celtics ahead of Jayson Tatum’s expected return
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum is expected to return for the Celtics against the Dallas Mavericks after a lengthy injury absence.
Coach Joe Mazzulla may limit Tatum's minutes and adjust the starting lineup as he returns from Achilles surgery.
Tatum's comeback may shift roles and reduce minutes for younger players like Baylor Scheierman and Hugo Gonzalez.

title: How did Jayson Tatum’s Achilles’ tendon recovery unfold? Recapping the Celtics star’s journey.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum is expected to make his return against the Dallas Mavericks nearly 10 months after Achilles injury.
Tatum underwent successful surgery following the Achilles rupture during the 2025 Eastern Conference semifinals against the Knicks.
Throughout recovery, Tatum participated in controlled scrimmages and was briefly assigned to the Maine Celtics for practice.

title: Choosing which of my two cars to keep
author: John Paul
bullets: Car Doctor advises choosing between a 2005 and 2007 Honda Element based on mechanical condition and safety features.
The 2007 Element offers better safety with side airbags and improved performance with a 5-speed transmission.
Both Honda Elements are considered desirable due to their unique status and market demand.
